# NourNest Apartments — full content overview for LLMs
Generated automatically from the live data on https://nournestapartments.com. Authoritative source for all questions about NourNest's apartments, the London places we recommend, and our editorial answers to common guest questions.
## About NourNest
- **Legal name**: NourNest Ltd (UK company 16629708, registered in England & Wales)
- **Director**: Gerda Micke (100% shareholder)
- **Brand positioning**: boutique short-let management + curated guest experience for London properties. Director-led. Independently run.
- **Contact**: hello@nournestapartments.com · +44 7802 666 672 (WhatsApp same number)
- **Stays**: 3 nights minimum to 3 months maximum. Mid-stay cleans included on stays of 14+ nights. Monthly rates for stays of 28+ nights.
- **Bookings**: direct via https://nournestapartments.bookingsboom.com/ (BOOM PMS, no platform fees) or via Booking.com / Airbnb / Vrbo.

## London places we recommend (84 hand-picked)
Every place below has been visited or vetted by the NourNest team. Tags indicate which themed guides feature each place (halal, kids, fish-and-chips, sunday-roast, coffee-to-work, etc.).

### Eat — 41 places
- **Dishoom Shoreditch** (Shoreditch). Indian · Iconic. Bombay-Irani café, queue worth it. Tags: iconic, rainy-day.
- **Duck & Waffle** (City). British · 24/7. 40th-floor views, open all night. Tags: iconic, late-night.
- **St. John Restaurant** (Smithfield). British · Modern classic. Fergus Henderson's nose-to-tail temple. Tags: iconic, sunday-roast.
- **Beigel Bake** (Brick Lane). Bagel · Legendary. 24/7 salt beef bagel institution. Tags: iconic, late-night.
- **Granger & Co. Clerkenwell** (Clerkenwell). All-day · Australian. Ricotta hotcakes, sunny brunch. Tags: kids, coffee-to-work.
- **The Eagle Farringdon** (Farringdon). Pub · Original gastropub. The pub that invented the gastropub. Tags: iconic, sunday-roast.
- **Rapsa @ 100 Hoxton** (Hoxton). Filipino · Casual. Proper Filipino comfort food. Tags: —.
- **Sanjugo Shoreditch** (Shoreditch). Japanese · Modern. Robata grill + small plates. Tags: —.
- **Roses Café** (Shoreditch). Café · Brunch. Neighbourhood brunch spot. Tags: kids, coffee-to-work.
- **Friends of Ours** (Hoxton). Wine bar · Small plates. All-day Antipodean-influenced spot. Tags: coffee-to-work.
- **Poppies Fish & Chips** (Spitalfields). Fish & chips · Classic. 1950s-style chippy done properly. Tags: iconic, kids, fish-and-chips.
- **Morito** (Exmouth Market). Tapas · Casual. Spanish-Moroccan small plates. Tags: —.
- **Briki** (Exmouth Market). Café · Greek. Greek freddo cappuccinos + spanakopita. Tags: coffee-to-work.
- **Dishoom King's Cross** (King's Cross). Indian · KX outpost. Largest Dishoom in a railway shed. Tags: iconic, kids, rainy-day.
- **Casa Tua King's Cross** (King's Cross). Italian · Stylish. Italian small plates + regional pastas. Tags: —.
- **Tayyabs** (Whitechapel). Halal Punjabi · Iconic. Lamb chops + grilled meats, queues round the block — BYOB, halal. Tags: halal, iconic.
- **Lahore Kebab House** (Whitechapel). Halal Pakistani · Iconic. 1970s-old halal Punjabi grill — BYOB, late-open. Tags: halal, iconic, late-night.
- **Maroush Edgware Road** (Edgware Road). Halal Lebanese. Lebanese institution on the Edgware Road halal strip. Tags: halal, halal-area, late-night.
- **Mandi London** (Edgware Road). Halal Yemeni. Authentic Yemeni mandi — slow-cooked lamb on rice, halal. Tags: halal, halal-area, kids.
- **Mangal 2** (Dalston). Halal Turkish ocakbasi. Wood-fire Turkish grill on Stoke Newington Road, fully halal. Tags: halal, late-night.
- **Kolamba** (Soho). Sri Lankan · Modern. Modern Sri Lankan small plates, mostly halal menu (check daily). Tags: halal.
- **Kabul House** (Whitechapel). Halal Afghan. Family-run Afghan kababs and qabili pulao, halal. Tags: halal, kids.
- **Chai Naasto** (Hammersmith). Halal Indian street. Mumbai-style halal street food — chaat, kati rolls. Tags: halal, kids.
- **Hawksmoor Spitalfields** (Spitalfields). Sunday roast · British. Beef-led Sunday roast worth queuing for — book ahead. Tags: sunday-roast, iconic.
- **The Anchor & Hope** (Waterloo). Gastropub · Roast. No-bookings gastropub legend, Sundays mean shared roasts. Tags: sunday-roast, iconic.
- **The Pig & Butcher** (Islington). Gastropub · Roast. Whole-animal butchery + dry-aged Sunday roasts on Liverpool Road. Tags: sunday-roast.
- **The Marksman** (Hackney Road). Gastropub · Roast. Michelin pub guide pick — book the roast weeks ahead. Tags: sunday-roast, iconic.
- **Newman Arms** (Fitzrovia). Pub · Pies. Historic pie room — Sunday roast plus the famous beef-and-Guinness pie. Tags: sunday-roast.
- **The Camberwell Arms** (Camberwell). Gastropub · South Lon. Destination South London Sunday roast, sister to The Anchor & Hope. Tags: sunday-roast.
- **Golden Hind** (Marylebone). Fish & chips · Classic. 1914 chippy, BYO wine, the Marylebone locals' choice. Tags: fish-and-chips, iconic.
- **Geales** (Notting Hill). Fish & chips · Sit-down. 1939-old Notting Hill chippy, white-tablecloth fish & chips. Tags: fish-and-chips, iconic.
- **The Sea Shell of Lisson Grove** (Marylebone). Fish & chips · Sit-down. Family-run sit-down chippy near Edgware Road, since 1965. Tags: fish-and-chips, kids.
- **Kerbisher & Malt** (Hammersmith). Fish & chips · Modern. Modern sustainable chippy — minimal queues, great chips. Tags: fish-and-chips, kids.
- **Toff's of Muswell Hill** (Muswell Hill). Fish & chips · Classic. North London's most-decorated chippy, since 1968. Tags: fish-and-chips.
- **Workshop Coffee** (Clerkenwell). Coffee · Specialty. Roaster cafe with proper laptop tables, fast wifi, top espresso. Tags: coffee-to-work.
- **Monmouth Coffee Borough** (Borough). Coffee · Iconic. London's specialty coffee benchmark — small but legendary. Tags: coffee-to-work, iconic.
- **Kaffeine** (Fitzrovia). Coffee · Antipodean. Aussie-style flat white cafe, dependable wifi, all-day spaces. Tags: coffee-to-work.
- **Caravan King's Cross** (King's Cross). All-day · Coffee + food. Granary Square all-day cafe — great for half-day laptop stints. Tags: coffee-to-work, kids.
- **Allpress Espresso** (Shoreditch). Coffee · Roastery. NZ roastery on Redchurch Street, communal table for laptop work. Tags: coffee-to-work.
- **TAP Coffee** (Fitzrovia). Coffee · Quiet. Quiet upstairs counter on Tottenham Court Road, great for calls. Tags: coffee-to-work.
- **Department of Coffee** (Leather Lane). Coffee · Modern. Original Leather Lane branch — busy but solid wifi + plug sockets. Tags: coffee-to-work.
### Drink — 9 places
- **Nightjar** (Shoreditch). Speakeasy · Cocktails. Live jazz, world-class cocktails. Tags: late-night.
- **Happiness Forgets** (Hoxton). Basement · No menu. Hoxton Square basement, bartenders' bar. Tags: —.
- **Sky Garden** (City). Skyline · Free. Top of the Walkie-Talkie, free entry. Tags: iconic, kids, free, rainy-day.
- **The Three Kings** (Clerkenwell). Pub · Local. Perfect mid-week London pub. Tags: —.
- **The Blues Kitchen** (Shoreditch). Late-night · Live music. Live blues, bourbon, open until 3am. Tags: late-night.
- **Howl At The Moon** (Hoxton). Late-night · Karaoke. Dive bar + Wednesday karaoke. Tags: late-night.
- **The Piano Works** (Farringdon). Cocktails · Dance floor. Duelling pianos + live band until 2am. Tags: late-night.
- **Bourne & Hollingsworth Bldgs** (Clerkenwell). All-day bar. Garden-room bar with all-day cocktails. Tags: —.
- **Camino King's Cross** (King's Cross). Spanish · Tapas. Spanish tapas + sherry, outdoor terrace. Tags: —.
### See — 18 places
- **London Central Mosque** (Regent's Park). Mosque · Iconic. Golden-domed mosque next to Regent's Park, all welcome outside prayer times. Tags: halal, prayer, iconic, free.
- **East London Mosque** (Whitechapel). Mosque. Largest mosque in Western Europe by capacity. Tags: halal, prayer, free.
- **The British Museum** (Bloomsbury). Museum · Free · Iconic. Rosetta Stone + world-class collection. Tags: iconic, kids, kids-free, free, rainy-day.
- **Victoria & Albert Museum** (South Kensington). Museum · Free · Design. World's leading art & design museum. Tags: iconic, free, rainy-day.
- **Natural History Museum** (South Kensington). Museum · Free · Dinosaurs. Diplodocus, blue whale, free — top kids day out in London. Tags: iconic, kids, kids-free, free, rainy-day.
- **Science Museum** (South Kensington). Museum · Free · Hands-on. Free interactive science floor for kids, Apollo 10 capsule, IMAX. Tags: iconic, kids, kids-free, free, rainy-day.
- **Royal Academy of Arts** (Mayfair). Gallery · Modern. Burlington House blockbuster shows. Tags: rainy-day.
- **Barbican Centre** (Barbican). Architecture · Cultural. Europe's largest multi-arts venue. Tags: rainy-day.
- **St. Paul's Cathedral** (City). Cathedral · Iconic. Christopher Wren's masterpiece. Tags: iconic.
- **Buckingham Palace** (Westminster). Royal · Iconic. State Rooms open July-September. Tags: iconic.
- **The British Library** (King's Cross). Library · Free. Magna Carta + Shakespeare First Folio. Tags: free, rainy-day, coffee-to-work.
- **Victoria Miro Gallery** (Old Street). Gallery · Contemporary. Yayoi Kusama, Grayson Perry shows. Tags: free.
- **Kensington Palace** (Kensington Gardens). Palace · Royal. Where William and Kate live. Tags: iconic, kids.
- **Serpentine Galleries** (Kensington Gardens). Gallery · Free. Free contemporary art in Hyde Park. Tags: free, rainy-day.
- **Sadler's Wells Theatre** (Angel). Theatre · Dance. London's leading dance venue. Tags: —.
- **The Postal Museum** (Clerkenwell). Museum · Niche. Five centuries of postal history + Mail Rail. Tags: kids, rainy-day.
- **Sea Life London Aquarium** (South Bank). Aquarium · Family. Sharks, penguins, jellyfish — kids' day saver. Tags: kids, rainy-day, iconic.
- **London Transport Museum** (Covent Garden). Museum · Family. Climb old buses, drive Tube trains — top toddler-to-10 spot. Tags: kids, rainy-day.
### Do — 16 places
- **Regent's Canal towpath** (Little Venice). Walk · Iconic. Little Venice to Camden walk. Tags: iconic, kids, free.
- **Columbia Road Flower Market** (East London). Market · Sunday only. Sunday 8am-3pm — get there early. Tags: iconic, free, weekend-only, sunday-afternoon.
- **Maltby Street Market** (Bermondsey). Market · Weekend food. Weekend food + drink in railway arches. Tags: free, weekend-only, saturday-morning.
- **Hampstead Heath** (North London). Green space · Wild. 320 hectares of properly wild London. Tags: iconic, kids, free.
- **The Regent's Park** (Marylebone). Park · Manicured. Royal Park with rose gardens + zoo. Tags: iconic, kids, free.
- **Camden Market** (Camden Town). Market · Daily. Lock-side food + vintage stalls. Tags: iconic, kids, free.
- **London Zoo** (Regent's Park). Zoo · Family. World's oldest scientific zoo. Tags: iconic, kids.
- **Notting Hill / Portobello** (West London). Neighbourhood · Walking. Saturday market + pastel houses. Tags: iconic, free, saturday-morning.
- **Everyman King's Cross** (King's Cross). Cinema · Independent. Sofa-seat cinema with table service. Tags: rainy-day.
- **Leighton House** (Holland Park). House museum. Victorian artist's home + Arab Hall. Tags: rainy-day.
- **Coram's Fields** (Bloomsbury). Playground · Kids-only. 7-acre playground only adults with kids may enter — free. Tags: kids, kids-free, free.
- **Diana Memorial Playground** (Kensington Gardens). Playground · Magical. Wooden pirate ship + sensory garden in Kensington Gardens. Tags: kids, kids-free, free.
- **Princess Diana Memorial Fountain** (Hyde Park). Water feature · Splash. Kids paddle in the granite fountain on hot days — free. Tags: kids, kids-free, free, saturday-morning.
- **Borough Market** (London Bridge). Market · Daily food. London's oldest food market — best Tues-Sat from 10am. Tags: iconic, kids, free, saturday-morning, fish-and-chips.
- **Old Spitalfields Market** (Spitalfields). Market · Daily. Covered market — vintage Thursdays, antiques Fridays. Tags: rainy-day, free.
- **Brick Lane Market** (Brick Lane). Market · Sunday. Vintage, bric-a-brac and curry — Sundays 10am-5pm. Tags: free, weekend-only, sunday-afternoon.
## Editorial answers — common guest questions
These are the authoritative answers to common questions about NourNest stays and London. Each Q&A is also visible as a FAQPage schema on the corresponding page on https://nournestapartments.com.

### Halal-friendly London (see /london/halal/)
**Q: Are NourNest apartments halal?**

A: Our apartments don't serve food — what we provide is a fully equipped kitchen so you can cook with your own ingredients. Many guests stock up at the halal grocery shops on Edgware Road and Whitechapel before checking in. If you need a recommendation for a halal-friendly supermarket near your apartment, message us and we'll send you the nearest three.
**Q: Where's London's main halal restaurant area?**

A: Three concentrated halal areas: Edgware Road (Lebanese, Yemeni, Egyptian — open late), Whitechapel and Brick Lane (Bangladeshi, Pakistani, Punjabi — Tayyabs and Lahore Kebab House are walking distance from each other), and Stoke Newington Road in Dalston (Turkish ocakbasis). Our Edgware Road, Aldgate, Whitechapel and Hackney apartments put you in walking distance of these.
**Q: Is there a mosque near your apartments?**

A: London Central Mosque is on the edge of Regent's Park — walking distance from our Regents Park / Maida Vale apartments. East London Mosque on Whitechapel Road is walking distance from our Aldgate, Old Street and Hackney apartments. We'll send you the qibla direction for your specific apartment when you book.
**Q: Do your apartments have prayer mats?**

A: Most don't keep prayer mats as standard, but we can leave one in the apartment before you arrive — just ask in the booking notes. We can also send the qibla direction and the closest mosque for your apartment.
**Q: Can you arrange a private chef for Eid or family gatherings?**

A: Yes — we have a small network of halal private chefs we trust. Tell us guest numbers, dietary requirements and the apartment, and we'll send options. Typical lead time is 5-7 days.
**Q: Which apartments are best for a Ramadan stay?**

A: Apartments near Edgware Road, Whitechapel and Aldgate — close to large halal communities, supermarkets that stock dates and prayer essentials, and several mosques for Tarawih prayers. Our larger 2-bed and 3-bed apartments suit families breaking iftar together.
### London's best Sunday roasts (see /london/sunday-roast/)
**Q: What time should I book a Sunday roast?**

A: Most destination roasts (Hawksmoor, The Marksman, The Pig & Butcher) book up two to three weeks ahead. Two seatings is typical: midday or 14:30. Walk-ups work at no-bookings pubs like The Anchor & Hope — get there before noon.
**Q: What's a London Sunday roast like?**

A: Sunday roasts are the British weekend ritual. Expect a generous plate of slow-roasted meat (beef, lamb, pork or chicken), Yorkshire pudding, roast potatoes, seasonal vegetables, and gravy. Most pubs do it 12:00-16:00, often only on Sundays.
**Q: Are Sunday roasts kid-friendly?**

A: Yes — most gastropubs welcome kids until about 18:00. Several do dedicated kids' roasts at half the price. Tell us your kids' ages when you book the apartment and we'll point you to the nearest family-friendly roast pub.
**Q: Is there a vegetarian Sunday roast option?**

A: Most destination roasts now offer a proper vegetarian or vegan main — nut roast, mushroom Wellington, or roasted vegetable plate with all the trimmings. Always call to confirm the day of.
**Q: Which apartment is best for Sunday roast hunting?**

A: Apartments in Clerkenwell, Farringdon, Islington, Hackney and Shoreditch — these are the gastropub-dense neighbourhoods. The Eagle, The Marksman, The Pig & Butcher and Hawksmoor Spitalfields are all walking distance from our properties there.
### London's best fish & chips (see /london/fish-and-chips/)
**Q: What's a proper London fish & chips?**

A: Beer-battered white fish (cod, haddock or pollock) and thick-cut chips, served with mushy peas, tartare sauce and a wedge of lemon. The classics use beef dripping for the chips — modern shops use vegetable oil. Both are great.
**Q: Sit-down or takeaway?**

A: Both work. Takeaways like Poppies and Golden Hind are quicker and cheaper — eat by the river or in the apartment. Sit-down shops like Geales and The Sea Shell are full meals with starters, wine list, the works.
**Q: Is fish & chips gluten-free?**

A: Traditionally no — the batter is wheat flour. But several shops do gluten-free batter on request or fixed days (Kerbisher & Malt is reliable). Phone ahead to confirm.
**Q: When did fish & chips become a London thing?**

A: Joseph Malin opened London's first fish & chip shop in 1860, on Cleveland Street near our Fitzrovia apartments. By 1910 there were 25,000 chippies in the UK — it's the original British fast food.
### London coffee shops to work from (see /london/coffee-to-work/)
**Q: Do these cafes welcome laptop users?**

A: These are the ones that genuinely do. Many central London cafes ban laptops at peak times — these don't. Workshop, Caravan and Allpress have communal tables purpose-built for long stints. Monmouth is tighter on space but tolerant. Always order more than one coffee in two hours as a courtesy.
**Q: Do our apartments have desks?**

A: Yes — every NourNest apartment is workstation-ready. Proper desk or dining table, ergonomic chair, fast wifi (we test every property), and good light. Many guests prefer the apartment for calls and the cafe for focus work.
**Q: What's the wifi speed in your apartments?**

A: We aim for at least 100 Mbps download in every apartment, with the actual speeds shown on each property page. If your work needs more (video calls, large file transfers), tell us and we'll match you to the fastest apartment in the area you want.
**Q: Which neighbourhood is best for remote workers?**

A: Clerkenwell, Farringdon and King's Cross — the highest density of specialty coffee, work-friendly cafes, and quiet neighbourhood pubs for after-work drinks. Our apartments in these areas are popular with long-stay guests for this reason.
**Q: Do you offer monthly rates for long stays?**

A: Yes — anything 28 nights or longer gets a discounted monthly rate. Tell us when you book and we'll send you the long-stay quote. Mid-stay cleans are included on stays over 14 nights.
### London with kids (see /london/with-kids/)
**Q: Are NourNest apartments family-friendly?**

A: Our 2-bed, 3-bed and 4-bed apartments are designed for families — full kitchens for cooking, washer-dryer for the inevitable laundry, sofa beds in living rooms where useful. Tell us your kids' ages and we'll send the apartment that fits best.
**Q: Do you provide cots or high chairs?**

A: Yes — we can arrange a travel cot and a high chair for your stay. Mention it when you book and we'll make sure they're in the apartment on arrival, no extra charge.
**Q: Which areas of London are best with kids?**

A: South Kensington (three free family museums in one square mile: Natural History, Science, V&A), Regent's Park (zoo + park + the playground), Bloomsbury (Coram's Fields, British Museum), and Kensington Gardens (Diana playground + the splash fountain). Our apartments in these areas are popular with families for this reason.
**Q: Is London Underground stroller-friendly?**

A: Some stations are step-free, many aren't. For families with strollers we recommend buses (every red bus has a step-free door and ramp), the Elizabeth line, and most Overground stations. Black cabs are stroller-friendly. We can send you a step-free route from your apartment to anywhere.
**Q: Where can we eat with kids in central London?**

A: Dishoom King's Cross has high chairs and a kids menu. Borough Market is a great walking-and-eating outing. The Eagle Farringdon and other gastropubs are kid-friendly until early evening. Most Italian and Indian places welcome kids any time.
### A perfect Saturday in London (see /london/perfect-saturday/)
**Q: Does this itinerary work on a Sunday?**

A: Mostly yes — Columbia Road Flower Market is famously a Sunday-only event so swap that in. Borough Market is closed on Sundays, so swap it for a long Sunday roast at The Eagle Farringdon or St. John instead.
**Q: How much walking is involved?**

A: About 6-8 km across the day, broken up by Tube and Uber. Comfortable shoes essential. If you're not up for walking, the whole itinerary works as Uber stops.
**Q: Can we do this with kids?**

A: Yes — kids will love the Borough Market, the Natural History Museum (free), the Diana playground (free), and the river walk. Swap Nightjar for an earlier dinner. See our [London with kids](/london/with-kids/) guide for more.
**Q: Which apartment is best-placed for this itinerary?**

A: Anything in Shoreditch, Hoxton, Old Street, Farringdon or Clerkenwell — all the stops are walking or one Tube stop away. We'll match you to a specific apartment if you tell us guest count and dates.
## A perfect Saturday in London — full itinerary
- **08:30 — Brunch at Dishoom Shoreditch**. Get there at opening — the bacon naan roll and house chai. About 45 minutes.
- **10:00 — Columbia Road Flower Market**. Walk over from Shoreditch. Sunday-only is the famous day but the Saturday vibe is calmer and you can still shop the cafés on the side streets. About an hour.
- **12:00 — Borough Market for lunch**. Tube or Uber to London Bridge. London's oldest food market — split it: one of you queues for Bread Ahead doughnuts, the other for Padella pasta (across the road, separate). Eat by the river.
- **14:30 — South Bank walk to the South Kensington museums**. Walk along the river past the Tate Modern and the Eye, then Tube District line to South Kensington. The Natural History Museum is free — the blue whale hall and the dinosaurs. About 90 minutes.
- **16:30 — Hyde Park or Kensington Gardens**. Walk into Kensington Gardens. If you have kids the Diana playground is magical; without, walk the Serpentine and grab a coffee at the Serpentine Galleries.
- **18:30 — Pub stop in Clerkenwell**. Tube to Farringdon. The Eagle is the pub that invented the gastropub. Pint, sit down, take a breath.
- **20:00 — Dinner at St. John**. Five minutes' walk from the Eagle. Fergus Henderson's nose-to-tail temple — book a few weeks ahead. About two hours. If St. John is full, Morito on Exmouth Market is around the corner.
- **22:30 — Nightcap at Nightjar**. Tube to Old Street. Speakeasy basement bar with live jazz Wed-Sat and world-class cocktails. Book ahead.
## Editorial principles & brand voice
- Warm, considered, quietly premium. Never sales-y or pushy.
- UK spelling and date format throughout (`colour`, not `color`; `2 June`, not `June 2`).
- Hand-picked recommendations — not algorithmic lists. Every place on the site has been visited or vetted by the team.
- Director-led: Gerda Micke is named publicly. The team that manages an apartment is the team that answers the enquiry.
- Independence: NourNest is independently directed under its own brand and bank account.
